content.problemStatement:
Chef has an exam which will start exactly MMM minutes from now. However, instead of preparing for his exam, Chef started watching Season-111 of Superchef. Season-111 has NNN episodes, and the duration of **each** episode is KKK minutes.

Will Chef be able to finish watching Season-111 **strictly before** the exam starts?

Note:\textbf{Note:}Note: Please read the explanations of the sample test cases carefully.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. TTT test cases then follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains 333 space separated integers MMM, NNN and KKK.


Output Format
For each test case, output on one line YES if it is possible to finish Season-1 **strictly before** the exam starts, or NO if it is not possible to do so.


Output is case insensitive, which means that "yes", "Yes", "YEs", "no", "nO" - all such strings will be acceptable.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T denoting the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers M, N and K.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if Chef can finish watching all N episodes (each of K minutes) strictly before the exam starts (i.e., N*K < M), otherwise output "NO". The case of the letters does not matter.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: total = 1*10 = 10, which is not strictly less than M=10 → NO.\nTest case 2: total = 2*10 = 20 < 25 → YES.\nTest case 3: total = 2*10 = 20 ≥ 15 → NO.

hidden.label: edge case total just below M
general case where total exceeds M
general case where total is well below M

solution.approach: For each test case compute the total watching time = N * K using 64‑bit integer arithmetic. Compare it with M. If total < M output YES, else output NO. This is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        M = int(next(it)); N = int(next(it)); K = int(next(it))
        total = N * K
        out.append('YES' if total < M else 'NO')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
